exampleSentencesTranslated: I have four close friends from university.
I want to buy four loaves of bread for breakfast tomorrow.
I will travel with four people in my family.
exampleSentences: Anh có bốn người bạn thân từ thời đại học.
Chị muốn mua bốn cái bánh mì cho bữa sáng mai.
Em sẽ đi du lịch cùng bốn người trong gia đình.
notes:
bốn = four (the number 4).

It’s a cardinal number, used before a classifier + noun.  

Vietnamese always needs a classifier when counting nouns, so don’t say bốn người bạn without the right structure.  
You’ll also see tư as a Sino-Vietnamese variant in fixed phrases like Thứ tư (Wednesday) or in some addresses.
notes_md:
**bốn** = four (the number 4).

It’s a cardinal number, used before a classifier + noun.  

Vietnamese always needs a classifier when counting nouns, so don’t say *bốn người bạn* without the right structure.  
You’ll also see **tư** as a Sino-Vietnamese variant in fixed phrases like *Thứ tư* (Wednesday) or in some addresses.
